questions.prompt: An ideal diode acts as:
choices: A. A short circuit when forward-biased, open circuit when reverse-biased
B. A constant 0.7 V drop always
D. An open circuit when forward-biased
questions.explanation: Ideal diode: short (0 V drop) when V_D > 0; open when V_D < 0. Real Si diodes have ~0.7 V forward drop.

questions.prompt: A silicon PN junction diode is forward-biased when:
choices: A. The anode is more positive than the cathode
B. The cathode is more positive than the anode
C. Both terminals are at the same potential
questions.explanation: Forward bias: anode (+) > cathode (−). Current flows from anode to cathode through the diode.

questions.prompt: The Shockley diode equation I = I_s(e^(V/V_T) − 1) at room temperature has thermal voltage V_T ≈
questions.explanation: V_T = kT/q ≈ 26 mV at 300 K (room temperature). This sets the scale of the diode I-V curve.

questions.prompt: A half-wave rectifier with a silicon diode and V_s = 10 V peak produces a peak output of approximately:
questions.explanation: V_out_peak = V_s − V_D = 10 − 0.7 = 9.3 V. One diode drop in the conduction path.

questions.prompt: A full-wave bridge rectifier uses ___ diodes and the peak output is:
questions.explanation: Bridge rectifier: 4 diodes, 2 conduct per half cycle → V_out_pk = V_pk − 2×0.7 = V_pk − 1.4 V.

questions.prompt: A Zener diode is specifically designed to operate in:
choices: B. The reverse breakdown region as a voltage regulator
questions.explanation: Zener diode maintains a nearly constant voltage V_Z in reverse breakdown; used for voltage regulation and reference.

questions.prompt: In a Zener voltage regulator, the Zener diode is connected:
choices: A. In series with the load
B. In parallel (shunt) with the load, cathode toward V+
C. In parallel with the series resistor
D. In series, anode toward V+
questions.explanation: Zener shunt regulator: Zener in parallel with load, cathode at top (V+). Current through series R; excess current through Zener.

questions.prompt: A Zener regulator: V_s = 12 V, V_Z = 5.1 V, R_s = 100 Ω, load R_L = 1 kΩ. Approximately what current flows through R_s?
A clipper circuit (with a diode) is used to:
choices: B. Clip (remove) a portion of the input waveform above or below a reference level
C. Shift the DC level of a signal
questions.explanation: Clipper: diode clips the signal above (or below) a threshold. Clamper: shifts the DC level.

questions.prompt: A clamper (DC restorer) circuit uses a diode and capacitor to:
choices: B. Shift the entire waveform up or down so a peak touches a reference level
questions.explanation: Clamper shifts DC level without distorting shape; capacitor charges to set the clamp level.

questions.prompt: The average (DC) output voltage of a half-wave rectifier with peak V_m is:
questions.explanation: Half-wave rectifier: V_dc = V_m/π ≈ 0.318 V_m. Full-wave: V_dc = 2V_m/π ≈ 0.637 V_m.

questions.prompt: A p-n junction diode in reverse bias has:
choices: B. A very small reverse saturation current I_s
questions.explanation: Reverse bias: depletion region widens; only small reverse saturation current I_s (~nA for Si) flows.

questions.prompt: The dynamic (small-signal) resistance of a diode at operating current I_D is:
questions.explanation: r_d = dV/dI = V_T/I_D from the Shockley equation at forward bias. At I_D=1 mA: r_d = 26 mV/1 mA = 26 Ω.

questions.prompt: An LED (light-emitting diode) emits light because:
questions.explanation: LED: forward-biased junction → electrons recombine with holes, releasing energy as photons (electroluminescence).

questions.prompt: A Schottky diode differs from a standard p-n diode in that:
choices: A. It has a higher forward voltage (~1.5 V)
B. It has lower forward voltage (~0.3 V) and very fast switching (no minority carrier storage)
C. It can only operate at RF frequencies
D. It conducts in both directions
questions.explanation: Schottky: metal-semiconductor junction, V_F ≈ 0.3 V, no minority carrier diffusion → extremely fast switching.

questions.prompt: Which types of diodes are designed for specific applications beyond simple rectification? (Select ALL that apply.)
questions.explanation: Zener: voltage reference; Varactor: voltage-tuned capacitor; Tunnel diode: quantum tunneling for negative resistance. Standard rectifiers are the baseline.

questions.prompt: The ripple factor (r) of a half-wave rectifier is approximately:
questions.explanation: Half-wave rectifier ripple factor r ≈ 1.21. Full-wave rectifier: r ≈ 0.48. Lower is better.

questions.prompt: The peak inverse voltage (PIV) across a diode in a half-wave rectifier is:
questions.explanation: Half-wave rectifier PIV = V_m (peak of source). Bridge rectifier PIV = V_m (each diode sees one V_m minus one diode drop).

questions.prompt: Forward-biasing a PN junction _____ the depletion region width.
questions.explanation: Forward bias opposes the built-in potential → narrows the depletion region → allows significant current flow.

questions.prompt: The diode equation I = I_s(e^(V/ηV_T) − 1) ideality factor η is typically:
questions.explanation: η (ideality factor) = 1 for pure diffusion current; 2 for recombination-dominated current. Typical: 1–2.

questions.prompt: A voltage doubler circuit uses capacitors and diodes to produce approximately:
choices: B. Twice the peak input voltage
D. The peak with polarity reversed
questions.explanation: Voltage doubler: capacitor-diode stages charge to V_m in successive half cycles, outputting ~2V_m DC.

questions.prompt: The small-signal equivalent circuit of a forward-biased diode consists of:
choices: B. A resistance r_d in parallel with a diffusion capacitance C_d
questions.explanation: Diode small-signal model: dynamic resistance r_d = V_T/I_D, plus diffusion capacitance C_d (from stored charge).

questions.prompt: A diode circuit has V_s = 5 V, R = 1 kΩ, and the diode drop V_D = 0.7 V. What is the diode current I_D in mA?
The average output of a full-wave rectifier (peak V_m) is:
questions.explanation: Full-wave rectifier: V_dc = 2V_m/π ≈ 0.637 V_m. (Half-wave: V_m/π ≈ 0.318 V_m.)

questions.prompt: Temperature increase in a silicon diode _____ the forward voltage drop.
questions.explanation: Si diode: V_F decreases ~2 mV per °C rise. This negative temperature coefficient is important for thermal runaway concerns.
